Article.ArticleTitle: Mechanism of sono-induced nitrosation side reactions and formation of toxic nitrosamine by-products: Kinetics and QSAR.

Abstract.AbstractText: The mechanism of inherent sono-induced nitrosation side reaction was investigated using model secondary amines as probes. For the first time, highly toxic nitrosamines were identified as specific sonication byproducts. A simplified kinetic model for nitrosamine formation (R2 = 0.894-0.998) indicated that the apparent degradation rate constants of secondary amines via nitrosation (k2,) followed a descending order of diphenylamine > dibutylamine > dipropylamine > diethylamine > piperidine > pyrrolidine > morpholine > methylethylamine > dimethylamine. Under the tested conditions, sono-induced nitrosation accounted for 0.75 %-35.66 % of the overall sono-degradation of secondary amines. The apparent maximum molar yield of nitrosamines (η) ranged from 0.15 % to 14.81 % of their precursors. The QSAR model of k2, (R2 = 0.998) revealed that logKow, pKa, and μ of secondary amines govern the nitrosamine formation, indirectly confirming that the sono-induced nitrosation predominantly occurs at the water-bubble interface. The maximum k2, and η values for each secondary amine were obtained at 600 kHz, and increased with increasing power densities. k2, values at different initial pH values followed the order of 7.0 > 11.0 > 3.0. PO43- and NH4+ inhibited sono-induced nitrosation, while Br- and HCO3-/CO32- promoted it. These findings elucidate sono-induced nitrosation side reaction mechanisms, enabling targeted control strategies.
